content: Management of drug-induced QT prolongation: (1) Discontinue offending agent(s); (2) Correct electrolyte abnormalities (maintain K+ >4.0 mEq/L, Mg2+ >2.0 mg/dL); (3) Avoid concomitant QT-prolonging drugs and CYP inhibitors that increase drug levels; (4) For TdP with hemodynamic instability, immediate defibrillation; (5) For stable TdP, IV magnesium sulfate 2g bolus followed by infusion; (6) Consider temporary overdrive pacing or isoproterenol to increase heart rate; (7) Monitor continuously until QTc normalizes. Prevention includes baseline ECG before starting high-risk drugs, follow-up ECG at steady state, avoiding multiple QT-prolonging agents, dose adjustment in renal/hepatic impairment, and monitoring drug levels when available. Patients with congenital long QT syndrome require special consideration.
